docs.title: PRAO of Wilmer
docs.text:
• When: Monday April 13th to (and including) Friday April 24th 2926 9:00-16:00 (see schedule)
  • Where in Uppsala: Biomedical Center, Husargatan: see location how to get there
  • Where in BMC: office B9:430b: see office how to get there
  • Things to bring: See the FAQ 'What should I bring?'
  • Schedule: see schedule
  • Report: see report
  • Reflection: see reflection
  • Reflection from supervisor: see Reflection from supervisor
  • Tips from previous interns

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: To find out how working life at NBIS/UPPMAX/UU is like.

docs.title: Project
docs.text: To be discussed in the first hour.

docs.title: Contact me
docs.text:
Contact me at

  • the email address shown at my GitHub account
  • by calling 079-3599417

docs.title: What should I bring?
docs.text:
• a laptop
  • (optional) food

docs.title: Where in Uppsala is this?
docs.text: At the Biomedical Center, Husargatan. See location how to get there.

docs.title: Where in the Biomedical Center must I be?
docs.text: At office D5:514c. See office how to get there

docs.title: Are there microwaves to heat my lunch food?
docs.text: Yes.

docs.title: What happens when I am early?
docs.text: You will make a great first impression and the PRAO will end earlier: that is one of the features of working at NBIS: we ourselves can determine when we work.

docs.title: What happens when I am late?
docs.text: You will make a bad first impression and the PRAO will end later: that is one of the features of working at NBIS: we ourselves can determine when we work.

docs.title: Location
docs.text:
We meet at the Biomedical Center in Uppsala at the Husargatan:

At BMC, we will meet at my office. See my office how to get there.

docs.title: Office
docs.text:
The office has room number D5:514c and can be found here (in Mazemap).

To reach my office, enter BMC at entrance C11:

BMC has another entrance (see below). Use C11.

After entering BMC entrance C11, in the hallways, take the stairs down.

Enter at entrance C11. In the hallways, take the stairs down

In the basement, turn right and follow the long corridor until the third square. You will see 'C5' painted in huge letters at the wall of the staircase. Take the elevator or stairs to floor 5.

turn right and follow the loooong corridor until the third 'square' Take the elevator or stairs to floor 5.

When arriving at floor 5, there are four easy chairs. We will meet there :-)

docs.title: What did you do?
docs.text: Prepared, taught, and followed a course about python on HPC clusters

docs.title: What did your supervisor do well?
docs.text: My supervisor was good at comunicating what I was supposed to do.

docs.title: How can your supervisor be improved?
What would you do differently next time?
docs.text: I would not do anything different.

docs.title: Which tip would you give to future PRAO students?
docs.text: Read thoroughly and listen carefully to all information given. Do your best and have fun!

docs.title: What I did well
docs.text:
• I treated Wilmer as a proper colleague
  • I took good care of Wilmer, always making sure he knew what to do
  • I kept the promises I made

docs.title: What I could do better
docs.text:
• I am unsure if I should have talked more informally to Wilmer. I love my job and it is a PRAO, so I tended to speak a lot about work. I do not know how to decide what is the right amount of informal talk
  • I am unsure how I handled talking about his -in my opinion- greatest weakness: giving an incorrect first impression, by showing too subtle emotions.

docs.title: My conclusion about my PRAO
docs.text:
• Wilmer is punctual: he always kept all his promises to meet at a specific place and time, typically being at least 15 minutes early
  • Wilmer can work a full day: he worked the maximum amount of hours (which is 7 hours per day), without ever complaining about this. He admitted that school is easier (as school has less hours) :-)
  • Wilmer is flexibel: he sometimes worked longer than the maximum amounts of hours on a day, so we had to schedule less hours on another day. This is quite normal at the place where I work and he was willing to go along with it
  • Wilmer is patient, even on tasks that are frustrating (i.e. getting access to HPC clusters and other administrative tasks)
  • Wilmer can focus and think: he was given tasks that require focus and thinking and he succeeded. He would not have been able to succeed without focus and deep thoughts. I never seen him being distracted by his phone.
  • Wilmer can give a better first impression: he rarely shows his emotions when not asked for it and the emotions he does show are subtle. People that know him better see these subtle signs. I hope this subtlety will not hinder him too much in the future, as people want to know if he likes/dislikes something. Two people misinterpreted his non-sharing of emotions as being not interested in the job. I think such an incorrect first impression is unhelpful

In conclusion:

Wilmer was an excellent colleague. I hope he will help others more to see this. I will strongly recommend him for any job that requires focus, deep thinking, patience and/or politeness.

docs.title: Goal
docs.text:
This paragraph is written by the supervisor

  • Prepare and assist teach HPC Python Day 1

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
Logged in to Tetralith, logged in to Pelle, checked links for correct documentation, logged in to Dardel, logged in to COSMOS, taught HPC python course day 1.

HPC Python Day 2: Interactive jobs and using IDEs, Birgitte Brydsö was speaking and showing how to do interactive jobs on other nodes than the login node. Second part was similar to day 1

docs.title: Tips from former students
docs.text:
Tips from former students, with spelling errors corrected.

Stay calm and focused and you will get it done, even if it feels stressful you'll get it done in the end. Remember the goal of a PRAO. The start is the hardest, it gets easier.

Emil

Make sure to get a good idea of what to do before starting so that you don't lose time due to misunderstanding the assignment.

Frans

Listen carefully to the explanations when you're given them. If you lose focus important information could be lost on you, and if you simply listen and take your time processing the info, a lot of issues can be avoided.

Noah

Take time to think through and plan the assignments beforehand so you have more time to spend writing and doing the assignment.

Johannes

Do your best! Don't give up, it might be hard at first but it gets better. Just belive in yourself and you will make it!

Signe

Ask the supervisor for extensive instructions

Olle

PLEASE ask questions when you don't understand. Don't ask the stupid AI, instead ask the smart human who is next to you at almost all the time: he knows better than AI trust me ;)

Max

Don't be shy to ask questions and don't have any distractions

Roza

Ask questions while being instructed/learned so that you can understand it better if there's some things you don't know.

Emil
